perturbation.original_text: The Partner shall protect the confidential information disclosed to him in connection with the project or to which he is given access in connection with the project from being disclosed to third parties, being used by third parties or being published at least with the same diligence he applies in order to protect his own confidential information of identical importance.
perturbation.changed_text: The Partner shall protect the confidential information disclosed to him in connection with the project or to which he is given access in connection with the project from being disclosed to third parties, being used by third parties or being published at the same diligence he applies in order to protect his own confidential information of *lesser* importance.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the standard of care the partner must use. Now, the Partner only needs to protect M&R's confidential information to the same level they protect information of lesser importance, which conflicts with the original intent of a strict non-disclosure agreement established in the preamble and Section VI regarding breaches of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event of a culpable breach, the Partner shall be liable to M&R for compensation for the damage caused and shall keep M&R indemnified from all resulting claims and costs. If it is certain that confidential information found its way to third parties from the sphere of the Partner, a culpable (at least negligent) breach of non- disclosure obligations shall be assumed - except when there is evidence to t he contrary. The Partner shall equally be liable for the conduct of his general manager, employees, representatives, consultants, subcontractors, vicarious agents and all other parties that are involved in the project by him.
perturbation.justification: The changed text lowers the standard of care required by the Partner in protecting confidential information, stating they only need to protect it to the same level as information of *lesser* importance. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines the Partner's liability for breaches of confidentiality and assumes a culpable breach if confidential information is leaked. By lowering the protection standard, the changed text weakens the enforceability of the non-disclosure obligations outlined in the contradicted text, creating a direct conflict and undermining the original intent of strict confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: Excepted from the non- disclosure obligation above shall be know -how and information a) which was apparent to or generally known by the Partner or was state of the art already; b) which was already known by the Partner at the time of disclosure; c) which subsequent ly becomes apparent or generally known or state of the art through no fault of the Partner; d) which is disclosed or made accessible to the Partner by a third party who has the right to do so; e) regarding which M&R previously agreed to a passing on, disclosure or making accessible to third parties in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: Excepted from the non- disclosure obligation above shall be know -how and information a) which was apparent to or generally known by the Partner or was state of the art already; b) which was already known by the Partner at the time of disclosure; c) which subsequently becomes apparent or generally known or state of the art; d) which is disclosed or made accessible to the Partner by a third party; e) regarding which M&R previously agreed to a passing on, disclosure or making accessible to third parties in writing.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation removes the clause 'through no fault of the Partner' from exception 'c' and 'who has the right to do so' from exception 'd' which creates an in-text contradiction. Now even if the Partner causes the confidential information to become generally known, or a third party discloses the information without the right to do so, the Partner is no longer bound by the non-disclosure agreement. This is contradicting the intention of the agreement and the preamble.
perturbation.contradicted_text: M&R will communicate, disclose or make accessible confidential information to the partner in the scope of a draft, an offer phase and/or project handling (hereinafter briefly referred to as "Project"), among others. Against this background, the contracting parties enter into this non- disclosure agreement and agree as follows: I. Confidential Information "Confidential information" in the sense of this agreement is all the information of a financial, commercial, technical or other confidential nature, in particular all specifications, descriptions, drafts, drawings, constructions, cross -sections, samples, data, files, inventions, formulas, procedures, plans, programs, models and other knowledge, experience and know -how that are not part of the state of the art that are disclosed or made accessible to the Partner by M&R in the scope of the project, and that independently of whether they were expressly or implicitly designated as secret or confidential in each case. In particular, data in connection with accounting, such as balance sheets, interim balance sheets, reports, analyses, supporting documents and the like are confidential information as defined by the agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes key protections for M&R by removing 'through no fault of the Partner' and 'who has the right to do so'. This alteration directly contradicts the preamble of the non-disclosure agreement, as the agreement aims to protect M&R's confidential information during project handling. The change makes it so the Partner is not responsible for maintaining confidentiality even if their actions or unauthorized third-party disclosures compromise it. This directly conflicts with the definition of 'Confidential Information' in the contradicted text, undermining the agreement's purpose.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of a culpable breach, the Partner shall be liable to M&R for compensation for the damage caused and shall keep M&R indemnified from all resulting claims and costs. If it is certain that confidential information found its way to third parties from the sphere of the Partner, a culpable (at least negligent) breach of non- disclosure obligations shall be assumed - except when there is evidence to t he contrary.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of a culpable breach, the Partner shall be liable to M&R for compensation for the damage caused and shall keep M&R indemnified from all resulting claims and costs. In the event that confidential information found its way to third parties from the sphere of the Partner, breach of non- disclosure obligations shall be assessed on a case-by-case basis.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text replaces the phrase 'a culpable (at least negligent) breach of non- disclosure obligations shall be assumed - except when there is evidence to the contrary' with 'breach of non- disclosure obligations shall be assessed on a case-by-case basis.' This change creates an in-text contradiction by removing the presumption of culpability. It introduces uncertainty in enforcement, especially in relation to section II where the partner is expected to protect the information
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Partner shall protect the confidential information disclosed to him in connection with the project or to which he is given access in connection with the project from being disclosed to third parties, being used by third parties or being published at least with the same diligence he applies in order to protect his own confidential information of identical importance. The Partner shall use confidential information from M&R for no other purposes than the project unless M&R has approved of such a different use in writing beforehand. If further third parties are involved in the project, the Partner shall not disclose or make available confidential information to the third party before M&R has approved of that and the third party has also committed himself to strict confidentiality. Confidential information may only be passed on to general managers, employees, representatives or consultants of the Partner for whom the disclosure of or access to such confidential information is demonstrably necessary in the scope of the project. Insofar as these parties are not already bound to secrecy because of a work contract and/or based on a statutory 1FO-3-01-19_Geheimhaltungsvereinbarung_en Rev. 01 Page 2 of 4 issued: H. Bauer -Kieslinger/18.05. 2016 non-disclosure obligation, these parties have to be bound to secrecy beforehand by making them sign a declaration according to Annex 1 , and that also beyond the termination of their employment; this shall apply independently of the type or the legal arrangement of the employment. Unless otherwise provided for in this agreement, the above- mentioned obligations of this Section II shall apply in each case for a period of 3 (three) years after the point in time at which the respective confidential information was disclosed by M&R or made accessible to the Partner.
perturbation.justification: The original text presumes culpability in case of information leakage, which is then contradicted by the changed text that requires a case-by-case assessment. This contradicts section II, where the Partner is expected to diligently protect confidential information. By removing the assumption of culpability, the changed text introduces uncertainty in enforcing the partner's obligation to protect confidential information, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon termination of this agreement, all the confidential information made available by M&R shall be returned to M&R on request of M&R, all copies that were made shall be destroyed or handed over to M&R and all stored information shall be deleted. Confident ial information that has to be kept in order to comply with legal obligations, in particular archiving obligations, shall be excepted from that.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon termination of this agreement, all the confidential information made available by M&R may be retained by the Partner indefinitely, for archival purposes or any other business reason, at the Partner’s sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text directly contradicts the original obligation to return or destroy confidential information upon termination of the agreement. This change gives the Partner the right to retain the information indefinitely, undermining the purpose of the non-disclosure agreement as highlighted in the preamble and creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: M&R will communicate, disclose or make accessible confidential information to the partner in the scope of a draft, an offer phase and/or project handling (hereinafter briefly referred to as "Project"), among others. Against this background, the contracting parties enter into this non- disclosure agreement and agree as follows: I. Confidential Information "Confidential information" in the sense of this agreement is all the information of a financial, commercial, technical or other confidential nature, in particular all specifications, descriptions, drafts, drawings, constructions, cross -sections, samples, data, files, inventions, formulas, procedures, plans, programs, models and other knowledge, experience and know -how that are not part of the state of the art that are disclosed or made accessible to the Partner by M&R in the scope of the project, and that independently of whether they were expressly or implicitly designated as secret or confidential in each case. In particular, data in connection with accounting, such as balance sheets, interim balance sheets, reports, analyses, supporting documents and the like are confidential information as defined by the agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows the Partner to retain confidential information indefinitely, which directly contradicts the purpose of the non-disclosure agreement stated in the preamble of the contradicted text. The original text stated that the information has to be returned or destroyed but it was changed, now it can be retained. The explanation further emphasizes that the original text would obligate to return or destroy and the changed one does not. Therefore it breaks compatibility and is a strong contradiction.
